options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: toadsanity
options.description: Add Toads as locations to be checked.

options.name: gold_mice
options.description:
Adds Gold Mice as locations to be checked.

Logic requires these to be obtained after blackout so they can't be missed. They can be acquired without black out, out of logic.

options.name: furnisanity
options.description:
Adds interactable objects, such as dressers, paintings, candles, and light fixtures, to the location pool.

Different sets of locations can be added within the list. Valid strings are:

"Hangables" includes items on walls such as paintings and other decor

"Decor" includes items such as instruments and suits of armor

"Ceiling" includes ceiling fans and lights attached to the ceiling

"Candles" includes any candles that can be interacted with

"Seating" includes chairs, stools and other typs of seating

"Surfaces" includes tables and other flat items

"Storage" includes things like barrels, boxes and shelves

"Plants" includes all waterable plants in the mansion

"Drawers" includes dressers, drawers, end tables and similar items

"Treasures" turns on only locations that contain treasure (including all plants) in the vanilla game. Does not create duplicate locations

"Basement, 1st Floor, 2nd Floor, Attic, and Roof can be used to turn on all furniture pieces on that level.

"Full" turns on all furniture locations and will override any other specified groups

options.name: boosanity
options.description: Turns Boos into Items and Locations.

options.name: portrification
options.description: Turn Portrait Ghosts into checks in addition to their clear chests.

options.name: silver_ghosts
options.description:
Adds locations on getting a Silver border for catching a portrait ghost.
Increasing Portrait Ghost health values will require Vacuum Upgrades in varying amounts

options.name: gold_ghosts
options.description:
Adds locations on getting a Gold border for catching a portrait ghost
Increasing Portrait Ghost health values will require Vacuum Upgrades in varying amounts

options.name: lightsanity
options.description: Adds the act of lighting up rooms as locations.

options.name: walksanity
options.description: Adds the act of visiting rooms as locations.

options.name: speedy_spirits
options.description: Adds Blue Ghosts to location pool. These have moved to be only available during blackout so they cannot be missed

options.name: WDYM_checks
options.display_name: What do you mean these are locations?
options.description: Adds locations you would never expect.

options.name: grassanity
options.description: Add grass locations to the location pool

options.name: rank_requirement
options.description:
Choose the required rank (H to A) to complete the game, with A being the highest.

Rank H requires any amount of money to finish the game, including 0

Rank A requires 5 Gold Diamonds (or equivalent money) to finish the game

options.name: game_mode
options.description:
Enable alternate game modes:

Default - Play through the mansion as usual

Poltergust Hunt - You must first find the Poltergust 3000 inside a piece of furniture before use. Enabling this will
automatically add certain pieces of furniture to the pool of locations even if other furniture options are turned off.
This will also unlock all doors in the mansion. This is intended as a short form game mode

options.name: vacuum_start
options.description: Enable Luigi to have the Poltergust 3000 at the start.

options.name: early_first_key
options.description: If enabled, this will attempt to find a key that lets you out of your starting room and place it early in your own world.

options.name: door_rando
options.description:
Various options regarding which doors are locked or unlocked in the mansion.

Off - Doors are in their vanilla state

Randomized - All doors are randomly locked or unlocked

Suit Doors - Randomize doors but guarantee the Suit Key Doors remain locked

All Doors Unlocked - Unlocks all doors in the mansion. Without Boo gates, this will make King Boo immediately accessible.

All Doors Locked - Locks all door in the mansion.

options.name: enemizer
options.description:
Choose if and how ghosts are randomized.

Vanilla - No ghost randomization

Randomized Elements - Randomized ghost elements and waves

No Elements - Remove ghost elements, randomize waves

options.name: random_spawn
options.description: Allows Luigi to randomly spawn somewhere in the mansion.

options.name: boo_gates
options.description:
Toggle the events that prevent progress unless a certain number of boos have been caught

Default to on. If this is turned off, the Boo Count options are ignored.

options.name: mario_items
options.description: How many Mario Items it takes to capture the Fortune-Teller. 0 = Starts Capturable

options.name: balcony_boo_count
options.description:
Set the number of Boos required to reach the Balcony. 0 = Starts Open

If Boosanity is off, this count may be reduced to make the seed viable, based on spawn location

options.name: final_boo_count
options.description: Set the number of Boos required to reach the Secret Altar. 0 = Starts Open

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: walk_speed
options.description: Choose how fast Luigi moves. Speeds above normal may cause OoB issues

options.name: vacuum_upgrades
options.description: Choose how many vacuum upgrades to include, up to 5.

options.name: boo_radar
options.description:
Start with Boo Radar

start_with: Start with Boo Radar

include: Boo Radar in pool

exclude: No Boo Radar - generation will fail if excluded while Boo Gates or Boosanity are enabled

options.name: hidden_mansion
options.description: Begin in the Hidden Mansion

options.name: enable_fear_animation
options.description:
Turn on Luigi being scared by ghosts if they spawn close to him.
Set this to false if you want to turn it off

options.name: enable_pickup_animation
options.description:
Turn on Luigi's pickup animations.
Set this to false if you want to turn these animations off.

options.name: luigi_max_health
options.description: Adjust Luigi's maximum health value, 1-1000. Values 5 or below should be treated as One-Hit KO

options.name: portrait_hints
options.description: Choose to add hints to the scans of the Portrait Ghosts' Hearts in the mansions

options.name: hint_distribution
options.description:
Choose the level of hint from in-game hints. Will affect Portrait Ghost hints if the option is on.

Junk hints will  not hint any items and instead provide funny lines

Strong hints will favor hinting progression items when choosing hints

Balanced will pick between progression items or other items to hint, but slightly favor progression items

Chaos wil pick items completely at random, with no favoring of progression or not

Vague hints will tell you the game the item is in, but not where

Disabled hints disables hint text completely

options.name: send_hints
options.description:
If enabled, in-game hints will be sent out to the multiworld when discovered.

This is automatically disabled if hint distribution is set to Junk, Disabled or Vague

options.name: self_item_messages
options.description: Choose whether you want all in-game received messages for your own items, only progression, or none at all.

options.name: trap_link
options.description: Games that support traplink will all receive similar traps when a matching trap is sent from another traplink game

options.name: enable_trap_client_msg
options.description: Enables messages in the client whenever a trap link is received.

options.name: energy_link
options.description:
Games that support energylink will be able to send and retrieve 'energy' from the team's pool.
If no team is present, the default team will be used (0).

'Energy' in the context of Luigi's Mansion will be money.

options.name: ring_link
options.description:
Games that support ringlink will be able to send and retrieve 'rings' sent from another ringlink game.

'Rings' in the context of Luigi's Mansion will be Coins.

options.name: enable_ring_client_msg
options.description: Enables messages in the client whenever a ring link is received.

options.name: king_boo_health
options.description: Set King Boo's health in the final fight.

options.name: boolossus_difficulty
options.description: Alter the difficulty of the mini-boos in the Boolossus fight. Easy slows them down, Hard speeds them up.

options.name: boo_health_option
options.description:
Choose how Boo Health is determined

Choice: Use Boo Health Value to set all boos to the specified value

Random Values: Every boo has a different, randomly chosen health value between 1 and the value set in Boo Health Value

Boo Health by Sphere: Boos will receive health values based on the spheres they are in. Boo Health Value will determine the highest health a Boo can have

Vanilla: No changes are made to Boos from the base game. Boos with HP over 150 may not be catchable within the current room and logic cannot account for where they move

options.name: boo_health_value
options.description:
Choose the health value all Boos will have if the Boo Health Option is Choice. Range between 1 and 999
If boo_health_option is set to random_values, if you set this to "100: 50", the max value used will be 100 instead.
If you want a custom range, use a random range function: https://archipelago.gg/tutorial/Archipelago/advanced_settings_en#random-numbers

Values over 150 may not be catchable within the current room and logic cannot account for where they move

If you have a boo go into an area you cannot access yet, you may need to save and quit to main menu to reload them.

options.name: boo_speed
options.description: Choose how fast boos move. Range from 1 to 36

options.name: boo_escape_time
options.description:
Choose how long before Boos leave the current room. Range between 1 and 300.

Values below 90 may not be catchable within the current room and logic cannot account for where they move

options.name: boo_anger
options.description: Choose whether boos can damage Luigi.

options.name: portrait_health_option
options.description:
Choose how Portrait Ghost Health is determined. Gold and Silver border locations will require upgrades if turned on
and Health is above certain values

Choice: Use Portrait Health Value to set all boos to the specified value

Random Values: Every Portrait Ghost has a different, randomly chosen health value between 1 and the value set in Boo Health Value

Portrait Health by Sphere: Portrait Ghosts will receive health values based on the spheres they are in. Portrait
Health Value will determine the highest health a Boo can have - this is capped by Vacuum upgrades in the pool

Vanilla: No changes are made to Portrait Ghosts from the base game.

options.name: portrait_health_value
options.description:
Choose the health value all Portrait Ghosts will have if the Portrait Health Option is Choice. Range between 1 and 999
If portrait_health_option is set to random_values, if you set this to "100: 50", the max value used will be 100 instead.
If you want a custom range, use a random range function: https://archipelago.gg/tutorial/Archipelago/advanced_settings_en#random-numbers

Higher values will require increasing amounts of vacuum upgrades. These will be forced on

options.name: extra_boo_spots
options.description: Adds extra spots for boos to hide in when furniture locations have been added to the pool

options.name: random_music
options.description: Randomize Music

options.name: door_model_rando
options.description: Randomly choose models for every door in the mansion.

options.name: chest_types
options.description:
Determines how chest colors and size are chosen,

vanilla: Chests are their original size and color

default: Size and color are determined by attempting to match the item type to a representative color similar to the vanilla game

full_random: Size and color are chosen completely at random.

color: Chest color represents the AP item classification

size_and_color: Both size and color are determined by AP item classification

no_fuzzy_matching: Same as default but uses item classification for other players' items

options.name: trap_chests
options.description:
Determines if chests containing traps can look like progression items

filler: Trap chests only appear as traps

progression: Trap chests only appear as progression

anything: Trap chests can appear as anything

options.name: call_mario
options.description:
Let everyone know you're looking for Mario in the client!
(CAUTION: THIS CAN EASILY SPAM THE CLIENT WITH MESSAGES)

options.name: spookiness
options.description:
Change ambient room sounds.
Vanilla = unchanged ambience
Full Spooky = All rooms become spooky
Random Rooms = Random rooms become spooky

options.name: filler_weights
options.description:
Set filler weights for filler items.
Each weight represents a number of balls in a lottery roller with that trap on it.
So if you had Banana Trap set to 3, and Ice Trap set to 7, and the rest set to 0,
you would have a 3/10 for a Banana Trap to be chosen when rolling for trap fillers
Must be between 0 and 100

options.name: trap_percentage
options.description: Set the percentage of filler items that are traps. Default percentage is 0%

options.name: trap_weights
options.description:
Set Trap Weights for traps chosen as filler items, if Trap Percentage is greater than 0
Each weight represents a number of balls in a lottery roller with that trap on it.
So if you had Banana Trap set to 3, and Ice Trap set to 7, and the rest set to 0,
you would have a 3/10 for a Banana Trap to be chosen when rolling for trap fillers
Must be between 0 and 100

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
